sources.title: It's a Great Life (MGM, 1929) THE DUNCAN SISTERS🍕 BACKSTAGE MUSICAL
sources.description:
PizzaFLIX Presents CLASSIC MOVIE MONDAY
Stars: Vivian Duncan, Rosetta Duncan, Lawrence Gray
Director: Sam Wood

Set in the world of musical revues, the film follows a pair of talented siblings—portrayed by Rosetta Duncan and Vivian Duncan, better known as The Duncan Sisters—navigating ambition, love, and loyalty as they try to make it big on stage. Known for its lively performances and early synchronized sound, the film blends comedy and music with the stylish energy of the Jazz Age. 

Lawrence Gray co-stars as the love interest caught between the two, while Benny Rubin and Jed Prouty add comedic flair to the supporting cast. 

Are you a PizzaFLIX Fanatic? Join our exclusive Fan Club for dedicated Classic movie lovers: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCE34fwgW7kWr7tc7YiOWtRw/join

🎭 Subscribe for more Oscar-winning films, vintage musicals, and Pre-Code Hollywood gems!

Adapted by Joseph Farnham from a story by Helen and Charles Kaufman at a time when Hollywood was racing into the talkie era, this production captures the sparkle and optimism of pre-Code musicals. With upbeat numbers, vintage fashion, and theatrical flair, it’s a feel-good time capsule of 1929 entertainment.

 #ItsAGreatLife #TheDuncanSisters #SamWood #MGM #RosettaDuncan #VivianDuncan #1920sCinema #PreCode #ClassicMusical #OldHollywood #PizzaFLIX

metadata.Plot: Casey and Babe are sisters who work in a department store and each year the store puts on a show. As expected, things are going wrong with every act until Casey comes out to help Babe with her song. They are a hit, but in the final act, Casey again comes out and this time the president sees her act and fires both her and Babe on the spot. Benny is able to book Casey, Babe and Dean into Vaudeville and their act is popular. But before they have their shot at stardom, Dean and Babe leave Casey and the act.
metadata.Writer: Byron Morgan, Leonard Praskins, Alfred Block
metadata.Actors: Rosetta Duncan, Vivian Duncan, Lawrence Gray
